title: Chapter 86 – Battle for Possession (5)
content:
When Jeong Yeon-shin spotted Flourishing Spirit Flower Master Namgung Mi from afar, he told Namgung Hwa-sin he would need ten seconds.

Even though his opponent was the Namgung young master, he measured his timing against Namgung Mi. They had already met once before.

He wondered whether he could break her spirit first.

The situation had shifted.

Just as he was about to carry out the martial matches according to the original mission, Namgung Mi had seized the narrative by linking even Virtuous Teacher Sect's servants with the evil sects.

She had turned the circumstances to her advantage, winning over Wuchang's public sentiment in the process.

Now it would be difficult to recklessly kill during the Desolate Sect versus Namgung life-and-death matches.

Because Desolate Sect warriors had to maintain the people's support at all times.

That was why he had taken out the pure blood robe and spoken of a mask. Jeong Yeon-shin gazed quietly at Namgung Hwa-sin.

"Mercy in methods..."

Namgung Hwa-sin trailed off, seemingly lost in thought for a moment.

"...However, the order has changed."

He raised his head. Distant sunlight swirled around his handsome features.

Opening his mouth with a face worthy of the title White Qilin, his eyes as they settled on Jeong Yeon-shin were firm.

It was the expression of someone genuinely worried for a companion.

"Namgung Mi is a direct-line rising talent. Judging by her energy waves, she seems to have recently consumed a peerless spirit medicine. The claim that she stands foremost among the younger generation may not be much of an exaggeration. Yin cold energy, freezing ice energy -- various terms exist, but yin-natured internal energy is extremely tricky."

Namgung Hwa-sin continued.

"They say the energy waves freeze your meridians first. It rapidly diminishes the fluidity of blood vessel circulation. Inner power release speed slows considerably, and explosive muscle strength plummets. The longer you exchange moves, the more your body grows dull. The power held by true qi specialized in the yin-yang five elements is truly frightening."

His tone was as grave as his expression. This was a matter of compatibility before martial arts level even entered the discussion.

Jeong Yeon-shin nodded.

"To have refined such internal energy into a spirit medicine... If the family's capability extends that far, I can understand the main family's scheme. Using Namgung -- with only its leadership toppled -- as a bulwark against the evil sects."

"Flourishing Spirit Flower Master Namgung Mi must have received cultivation techniques suited to absorbing the spirit medicine. Or perhaps they brought in trusted internal masters from outside. A family with that kind of power would have the means."

Influence and wealth, or sheer martial might. They must have used every resource available to raise their only two direct-line members.

"Skipping the energy accumulation process entirely with spirit medicine..."

Jeong Yeon-shin muttered.

Accumulation. The gathering of energy. The martial world was full of practitioners who spent long years building true qi through heart techniques, energy circulation, and absorption methods.

It was common to say that training one full heavenly stem-earthly branch cycle required sixty years.

When martial artists spoke of gaining enlightenment in heart techniques, they meant discovering thought methods better suited to energy absorption than their previous ones.

'Always lacking in energy accumulation.'

It had been that way even after consuming the supply spirit pill said to be of superior grade.

Even Namgung Hwa-sin, standing right before him, possessed deeper internal energy. It was a difference born of innate limits.

That was precisely why Jeong Yeon-shin now strove to create cultivation techniques with faster energy accumulation.

"Not just skipping the process, but gaining even greater strength."

Namgung Hwa-sin's expression turned bitter as he looked away.

He continued.

"It's the strength of a family that exists solely for the direct line. Different from Desolate Sect. Peerless spirit medicines passing through the main fortress are funneled to the imperial family. Though it all happens in utmost secrecy."

"...Even the spirit pill I received was no small thing."

"This would be far beyond that. Without question."

Perhaps because he was contemplating his half-sister? Namgung Hwa-sin's lips trembled again.

"Flourishing Spirit Flower Master and Azure Qilin are no different from great trees nurtured by the mountain that is the Namgung family. I'm not dismissing you, Young Hero Jeong. I mean there are martial arts difficult to counter even at equal ranking. Starting with me. Though I volunteered for this mission to overcome my heart demons, I'm far from certain I can overcome them in time."

He clasped his own trembling hands. Namgung Hwa-sin smiled awkwardly.

'This mission carries several meanings.'

Jeong Yeon-shin thought.

The Desolate Sect main fortress had issued the mission to govern the murim under heaven. Jeong Yeon-shin, whose life thread hung in the balance, had volunteered to accumulate achievements. And Namgung Hwa-sin, long despised, had come to shed the weight of past years and avenge his mother.

'During the competition, he tried to defeat me so he could face the young master himself. Does he hold a grudge against Azure Qilin as well?'

The boy studied Namgung Hwa-sin steadily.

After gazing at him for a moment, he slowly parted his lips.

"Though I can't lift Young Hero Namgung's spirits..."

"Haha, I don't expect such things from Young Hero Jeong."

After Namgung Hwa-sin's quiet laugh, Jeong Yeon-shin continued.

"It seems I can pull Namgung Mi down."

* * *

The woman with snow-white skin slowly bit into a peach.

The chair she occupied was draped in luxurious tiger skin, and her appearance -- hands resting on the armrests like a monarch's -- suited her perfectly.

Moving her small mouth without hurry, she spoke.

"Do it."

Namgung Mi's command fell. For some, those words announced their end.

The bodies of those remaining at Wuchang's Virtuous Teacher Sect trembled with uncontrollable fear.

The servants had already been crippled. Next came the attendants.

The Namgung family's Flourishing Spirit Flower warriors approached. Energy waves radiated from their bare hands.

Their eyes as they looked down at the attendants were completely indifferent. Not the gazes of people regarding equals.

"Save us, please save us!"

"I was dragged here!"

Ming dynasty law did not permit private torture.

In Great Ming, commoners were the emperor's property. No one was to defy this.

However, the Central Plains were vast -- far too vast for the imperial court to scrutinize thoroughly.

At this moment, Namgung Mi's orders stood above Great Ming law. All the more so within the Namgung family's territory.

"I won't kill you."

She smiled thinly, a smile carrying even a measure of dignity.

"I'll simply destroy your dantians. You learned vicious evil sect techniques. Since you served as dogs for this wicked Virtuous Teacher Sect, who knows what atrocities you might commit against commoners once your fallen sect is gone?"

"Never learned martial arts... HEOK!"

The moment the Flourishing Spirit Flower warriors moved, screams erupted from every direction.

The pain of dantian destruction is no trifling thing. It meant tearing apart the Ocean of Qi point in the lower abdomen with internal energy palm technique.

How could anyone endure the sensation of blood vessels being severed completely? Never could they be made whole again.

Over a dozen attendants writhed on the ground. Blood came from below and from their mouths simultaneously.

Bloodstains spread across the floor of Virtuous Teacher Sect's central hall.

Some of the Virtuous Teacher Sect workers had been fortunate enough to be selected as Namgung Mi's servants.

They stood off to one side, not daring to make a sound. Rigid faces. They could barely even breathe.

"Clean up and summon the people. I'll hold a feast to distribute this insignificant evil sect's wealth."

Namgung Mi said.

Within the Namgung family's domain, her word was law. What she spoke came to pass immediately.

It was sunset. The early summer sun stretched the twilight's orange light long across the ground, elongating the people's shadows.

Under one Flourishing Spirit Flower warrior's watch, the attendants could not act rashly. Virtuous Teacher Sect's practice ground was soon transformed into a banquet hall for the people of Wuchang.

The Namgung family's masters had exterminated Virtuous Teacher Sect. This was no ordinary news.

Local dignitaries gathered of their own accord, without invitation.

"So satisfying!"

"Serves those Virtuous Teacher Sect bastards right!"

"A Snow Mountain fairy has descended bearing the Namgung name. Just look at that jade-like skin. She's not of common blood."

"And possessing accomplished martial arts, no less. Truly something to be grateful for."

The table brought out from inside Virtuous Teacher Sect was massive. Dozens of people sat around eating and drinking.

They were primarily owners of merchant groups, escort agencies, and martial arts halls established in Wuchang.

Even the head of Wuchang's government school stroked his beard respectfully.

"So, you're saying a Namgung family branch will be established here?"

A middle-aged bald man asked. Though he claimed to head a mid-sized escort agency, his manner was that of a subordinate addressing a superior.

Namgung Mi slowly nodded.

"You saw Virtuous Teacher Sect's deplorable conduct. Though it is inadequate, I wish to oversee the murim's security in Wuchang..."

The corners of her mouth gently curled upward.

"I hope I won't be burdening you all."

"Absolutely not!"

"Rather, it's something to be thankful for!"

Many in attendance waved their hands. This was the Namgung family's precious daughter they were dealing with.

Even if they truly disliked it, they had no choice but to agree.

"Mm, thank you."

The smile on Namgung Mi's lips deepened. A young martial artist who had been staring blankly at her raised his hand.

"Young Lady Namgung! I heard you'll be competing with Desolate Sect warriors!"

"Oh my, are you worried?"

When she smiled as if delighted, he flushed crimson. It was a reaction that never grew old.

Such was the martial world Namgung Mi had known.

Though various imperial princes and princesses with elf blood were said to be precious, she -- who had grown up as a clan member no less than royalty in the free world of the murim -- did not envy them.

She considered herself to have the cultivation necessary to be content with what she possessed. She only wanted to protect what was hers.

Namgung Mi tilted her head slightly to the side. Her glossy hair shifted with the movement. It was deliberate.

"I wonder why Desolate Sect left Virtuous Teacher Sect alone? When so many commoners were suffering."

Those present immediately caught her meaning. Anyone would have known what words she was fishing for.

"They're just desperate to strangle the murim. How would we know their true intentions?"

"They must have left them be because it suited them! Since Wuchang's orthodox factions couldn't raise their heads because of those evil sect bastards! Isn't Desolate Sect just a group of martial artists oppressing the murim? Just look at the Hwangbo family incident..."

"They sent life-and-death challenge letters to Namgung, who fought fierce battles with Blood Flame Sect. They trample murim principles into the ground."

Their denunciations were fervent. They seemed to genuinely believe what they said.

Namgung Mi watched them steadily. These various martial artists were no different from commoners.

They curried favor with whoever was stronger. Surely some held different views.

It didn't matter. Her martial world was like this.

None who deny their own survival. A world spreading beautifully like a carpet.

That was why she wanted to protect it.

That was why the family elders had overlooked the Desolate Sect's Lightning Genius who had appeared wielding remarkable yin techniques.

It would have been different had he been a rising disciple of the Orthodox Schools. Survival of the fittest.

To the eyes of a family expanding its influence through the ways of the murim, Desolate Sect was an exasperating group.

They interfered in murim matters at every turn, armed with nothing but the justification of civilian welfare.

'Must cut off the sprout. This time, for certain.'

She had won the battle for justification with ease. Seized Wuchang's public sentiment in an instant.

Desolate Sect, the ones who had proposed life-and-death matches, were the ones who had to mind the eyes of all the people.

Though she would not lose even in straightforward martial matches, as a daughter of a major murim faction, she did everything within her power.

'The careful planner is the strong one in this world.'

She was reaching for a peach at one end of the table, still wearing her gentle smile, when it happened.

"Who."

Every hand froze. That was not a voice to let pass. The sheer bearing it carried was tremendous.

"Spoke of our sect."

A voice rang out -- boyish in timbre yet laced with the rough, rupturing sounds of true qi.

It seemed to mix inner power at the Heaven's Edge point directly above the vocal cords.

Accompanied by the sound of fluttering cloth, a red figure crossed the wall. The body technique was extraordinarily accomplished.

STEP.

With light footsteps, the figure descended into the banquet hall. Not even a speck of dust was raised.

Namgung Mi immediately deployed her eye techniques and raised her gaze.

Her eyes, carrying the sky-colored radiance of the Namgung family's secret Azure Sky Eye Technique, swept the figure from head to toe.

She was confident she could detect even the smallest tremor of their body.

The uninvited guest who had said "our sect."

Wearing a demon mask grinning like a gaping dragon.

Even the energy waves swirling like wind wrapped around the bright red robe's sleeves, hanging down to the wrists. The presence was overwhelming from the start.

"Blood... Flame Sect?"

Someone's dazed muttering reached her ears.

Namgung Mi did not take her eyes off them.

The ominous waves were unusual. The flow brushing against her skin like ripples spoke of exceptionally delicate true qi manipulation.

The power control was terrifying. It did not look at all like the figure was displaying their full innate inner power.

"You."

The red-robed demon mask spoke.

"Are you the Namgung bastard?"

In that moment, no one opened their mouths. It was truly a shocking entrance.
